passage_title: A second attempt at domesticating the tomato

sentences.sentence: It took at least 3,000 years for humans to learn how to domesticate the wild tomato and cultivate it for food.
sentences.translation: 人类至少花了3000年才学会如何驯化野生番茄，并将其作为食物种植。

structure.text: for humans to learn how to domesticate the wild tomato and cultivate it for food
how to domesticate the wild tomato and cultivate it for food

difficult_points: It took ... for humans to learn ... 是形式主语结构，真正说明耗时内容的是后面的不定式复合结构。
how to domesticate ... and cultivate ... 中两个动词并列，共同说明人类学会的内容。
it 指代前面的 the wild tomato，不是泛指其他食物。

sentences.sentence: Now two separate teams in Brazil and China have done it all over again in less than three years.
sentences.translation: 如今，巴西和中国的两个独立研究团队在不到三年的时间里又重新完成了这一过程。

structure.text: two separate teams in Brazil and China
have done it all over again

difficult_points: have done 使用现在完成时，强调这项重新驯化已经完成并与现在相关。
it 指代上一句中驯化野生番茄这件事。
all over again 表示“重新再做一遍”，突出与3000年历史过程的对比。

sentences.sentence: And they have done it better in some ways, as the re-domesticated tomatoes are more nutritious than the ones we eat at present.
sentences.translation: 而且从某些方面看，他们做得更好，因为这些被重新驯化的番茄比我们目前吃的番茄更有营养。

structure.text: have done it better in some ways
as the re-domesticated tomatoes are more nutritious than the ones we eat at present

difficult_points: as 引导原因状语从句，解释为什么说研究团队“做得更好”。
the ones 指代 tomatoes，后面的 we eat at present 是省略关系代词的定语从句。
more nutritious than 构成比较结构，将重新驯化的番茄与当前食用番茄比较。

sentences.sentence: This approach relies on the revolutionary CRISPR genome editing technique, in which changes are deliberately made to the DNA of a living cell, allowing genetic material to be added, removed or altered.
sentences.translation: 这种方法依赖革命性的CRISPR基因组编辑技术；在这项技术中，人们有意改变活细胞的DNA，从而使遗传物质能够被添加、移除或修改。

structure.text: relies on the revolutionary CRISPR genome editing technique
in which changes are deliberately made to the DNA of a living cell
allowing genetic material to be added, removed or altered

difficult_points: in which 引导非限制性定语从句，which 指代 CRISPR genome editing technique。
changes are deliberately made 是被动语态，强调DNA变化是人为有意进行的。
allowing ... 是现在分词结构，说明前面DNA改变带来的结果。
to be added, removed or altered 是并列被动不定式，三个动作共同修饰 genetic material。

sentences.sentence: The technique could not only improve existing crops, but could also be used to turn thousands of wild plants into useful and appealing foods.
sentences.translation: 这项技术不仅能够改良现有作物，还可以被用来把数千种野生植物变成有用且有吸引力的食物。

structure.text: could not only improve existing crops
but could also be used to turn thousands of wild plants into useful and appealing foods
to turn thousands of wild plants into useful and appealing foods

difficult_points: not only ... but also ... 连接两个并列谓语，说明这项技术的两种潜在用途。
could be used 是情态动词加被动语态，表示可能用途而非已经全部实现。
turn ... into ... 表示“把……变成……”，宾语是 thousands of wild plants。

sentences.sentence: In fact, a third team in the US has already begun to do this with a relative of the tomato called the groundcherry.
sentences.translation: 事实上，美国的第三个团队已经开始用番茄的一种近缘植物——地樱桃——来做这件事。

structure.text: a third team in the US
has already begun to do this
with a relative of the tomato called the groundcherry

difficult_points: has already begun 使用现在完成时，表示行动已经开始并延续到当前背景。
this 指代前一句中把野生植物变成有用食物的做法。
called the groundcherry 是过去分词后置定语，修饰 a relative of the tomato。

sentences.sentence: This fast-track domestication could help make the world's food supply healthier and far more resistant to diseases, such as the rust fungus devastating wheat crops.
sentences.translation: 这种快速驯化可能有助于让世界粮食供应更加健康，并且更能抵抗疾病，例如正在严重破坏小麦作物的锈菌病。

structure.text: could help make the world's food supply healthier and far more resistant to diseases
healthier and far more resistant to diseases
such as the rust fungus devastating wheat crops

difficult_points: make the world's food supply healthier ... 是 make 加宾语加宾补结构。
healthier 和 far more resistant 并列，分别说明粮食供应的健康性和抗病性。
devastating wheat crops 是现在分词后置定语，修饰 the rust fungus。

sentences.sentence: 'This could transform what we eat,' says Jorg Kudla at the University of Munster in Germany, a member of the Brazilian team.
sentences.translation: 巴西团队成员、德国明斯特大学的Jorg Kudla说：“这可能会改变我们的饮食内容。”

structure.text: a member of the Brazilian team

difficult_points: what we eat 是宾语从句，作 transform 的宾语，意思是“我们吃的东西”。
says Jorg Kudla 是倒装式引述句，主语在动词后。
a member of the Brazilian team 是同位语，补充说明 Jorg Kudla 的身份。

sentences.sentence: 'There are 50,000 edible plants in the world, but 90 percent of our energy comes from just 15 crops.'
sentences.translation: “世界上有5万种可食用植物，但我们90%的能量仅来自15种作物。”

structure.text: There are 50,000 edible plants in the world
but 90 percent of our energy comes from just 15 crops

difficult_points: There are 是存在句，先说明可食用植物数量很多。
but 连接转折，突出植物总数与人类能量来源高度集中的反差。
just 15 crops 中 just 强调“仅仅”，不是时间意义。

sentences.sentence: 'We can now mimic the known domestication course of major crops like rice, maize, sorghum or others,' says Caixia Gao of the Chinese Academy of Sciences in Beijing.
sentences.translation: 中国科学院北京的高彩霞说：“我们现在可以模拟水稻、玉米、高粱等主要作物已知的驯化过程。”

structure.text: can now mimic the known domestication course of major crops like rice, maize, sorghum or others,'
like rice, maize, sorghum or others
says Caixia Gao of the Chinese Academy of Sciences in Beijing

difficult_points: mimic 在这里表示“模拟、仿照”，不是简单的“模仿动作”。
the known domestication course 指已有主要作物被驯化的路径或过程。
like 后列举 rice, maize, sorghum 等 major crops 的例子。

sentences.sentence: 'Then we might try to domesticate plants that have never been domesticated.'
sentences.translation: “然后我们或许可以尝试驯化那些从未被驯化过的植物。”
difficult_points: might 表示推测性的可能，不是确定承诺。
that 引导定语从句，修饰 plants。
have never been domesticated 是现在完成时被动语态，强调这些植物至今从未被驯化。

sentences.sentence: Wild tomatoes, which are native to the Andes region in South America, produce pea-sized fruits.
sentences.translation: 野生番茄原产于南美洲的安第斯地区，会结出豌豆大小的果实。

structure.text: which are native to the Andes region in South America

difficult_points: which 引导非限制性定语从句，补充说明 wild tomatoes 的原产地。
are native to 表示“原产于”，to 后接地点。
pea-sized 是复合形容词，说明 fruits 的大小。

sentences.sentence: Over many generations, peoples such as the Aztecs and Incas transformed the plant by selecting and breeding plants with mutations* in their genetic structure, which resulted in desirable traits such as larger fruit.
sentences.translation: 经过许多代人，阿兹特克人和印加人等民族通过选择并培育遗传结构中带有突变的植株，改变了这种植物，这些突变带来了诸如果实更大等理想性状。

structure.text: peoples such as the Aztecs and Incas
by selecting and breeding plants with mutations* in their genetic structure
which resulted in desirable traits such as larger fruit

difficult_points: by selecting and breeding ... 说明 transformed the plant 的方式。
with mutations* in their genetic structure 是介词短语，修饰 plants。
which 指代前面突变这一情况，引出其结果是产生 desirable traits。
such as larger fruit 举例说明 desirable traits 的具体内容。

sentences.sentence: But every time a single plant with a mutation is taken from a larger population for breeding, much genetic diversity is lost.
sentences.translation: 但是，每当为了育种从更大的种群中取出一株带有突变的植株时，许多遗传多样性就会丧失。

structure.text: every time a single plant with a mutation is taken from a larger population for breeding

difficult_points: every time 引导时间状语从句，表示“每当……”。
a single plant with a mutation is taken 是被动结构，说明被选走的是一株突变植物。
much genetic diversity is lost 是主句，也用被动语态说明结果。

sentences.sentence: And sometimes the desirable mutations come with less desirable traits.
sentences.translation: 而且，有时理想的突变也会伴随不那么理想的性状。
difficult_points: And 在句首承接上一句，继续说明传统育种带来的问题。
come with 在这里表示“伴随产生”，不是空间上的“和……一起来”。
desirable 与 less desirable 形成对照，说明有利突变可能同时带来不利性状。

sentences.sentence: For instance, the tomato strains grown for supermarkets have lost much of their flavour.
sentences.translation: 例如，为超市种植的番茄品系已经失去了许多风味。

structure.text: the tomato strains grown for supermarkets
have lost much of their flavour

difficult_points: For instance 引出上一句“不理想性状”的具体例子。
grown for supermarkets 是过去分词后置定语，修饰 tomato strains。
have lost 使用现在完成时，表示风味丧失的结果延续到现在。

sentences.sentence: By comparing the genomes of modern plants to those of their wild relatives, biologists have been working out what genetic changes occurred as plants were domesticated.
sentences.translation: 通过将现代植物的基因组与其野生近缘植物的基因组进行比较，生物学家一直在弄清植物被驯化时发生了哪些遗传变化。

structure.text: By comparing the genomes of modern plants to those of their wild relatives

difficult_points: By comparing ... 是介词加动名词短语，说明研究方法。
those 指代 genomes，避免重复 the genomes。
have been working out 是现在完成进行时，强调研究工作从过去持续到现在。
what genetic changes occurred 是宾语从句，作 working out 的宾语。

sentences.sentence: The teams in Brazil and China have now used this knowledge to reintroduce these changes from scratch while maintaining or even enhancing the desirable traits of wild strains.
sentences.translation: 巴西和中国的团队现在已经利用这些知识，从零开始重新引入这些变化，同时保留甚至增强野生品系的理想性状。

structure.text: The teams in Brazil and China
to reintroduce these changes from scratch
while maintaining or even enhancing the desirable traits of wild strains

difficult_points: have now used 是现在完成时，说明研究团队已经把知识用于实践。
to reintroduce ... 表示使用这些知识的目的。
from scratch 表示“从零开始”，呼应前文重新驯化。
while maintaining or even enhancing ... 是省略主语的伴随结构，说明重新引入变化的同时保留或增强性状。

sentences.sentence: Kudla's team made six changes altogether.
sentences.translation: Kudla的团队总共做出了六项改变。
difficult_points: made six changes 表示团队进行了六处基因层面的改动。
altogether 强调总数，为后文举例说明这些改动做铺垫。

sentences.sentence: For instance, they tripled the size of fruit by editing a gene called FRUIT WEIGHT, and increased the number of tomatoes per truss by editing another called MULTIFLORA.
sentences.translation: 例如，他们通过编辑一个名为FRUIT WEIGHT的基因，使果实大小增加到三倍；又通过编辑另一个名为MULTIFLORA的基因，增加了每个果穗上的番茄数量。

structure.text: tripled the size of fruit by editing a gene called FRUIT WEIGHT
and increased the number of tomatoes per truss by editing another called MULTIFLORA

difficult_points: tripled 是动词，表示“使……增加到三倍”。
by editing ... 两次出现，分别说明实现两个改动的方式。
another 指 another gene，后面省略了 gene。
called FRUIT WEIGHT 和 called MULTIFLORA 都是过去分词后置定语。

sentences.sentence: While the historical domestication of tomatoes reduced levels of the red pigment lycopene – thought to have potential health benefits – the team in Brazil managed to boost it instead.
sentences.translation: 虽然历史上的番茄驯化降低了红色色素番茄红素的含量——这种色素被认为可能有健康益处——但巴西团队反而成功提高了它的含量。

structure.text: While the historical domestication of tomatoes reduced levels of the red pigment lycopene
thought to have potential health benefits

difficult_points: While 引导让步/对比从句，将历史驯化的结果与巴西团队的结果对比。
thought to have potential health benefits 是插入的过去分词短语，修饰 lycopene。
managed to boost it 强调成功提高含量，it 指代 lycopene。
instead 表示结果与前面的 reduced levels 相反。

sentences.sentence: The wild tomato has twice as much lycopene as cultivated ones; the newly domesticated one has five times as much.
sentences.translation: 野生番茄的番茄红素含量是栽培番茄的两倍；新近驯化的番茄则是其五倍。

structure.text: has twice as much lycopene as cultivated ones

difficult_points: 分号连接两个并列分句，分别比较野生番茄和重新驯化番茄的番茄红素含量。
twice as much ... as ... 是倍数比较结构。
cultivated ones 和 the newly domesticated one 中的 one/ones 都指 tomato/tomatoes。

sentences.sentence: 'They are quite tasty,' says Kudla.
sentences.translation: Kudla说：“它们相当好吃。”
difficult_points: They 指代前文中重新驯化的番茄。
quite tasty 是口语化评价，说明研究结果不仅营养提升，也保留了可食用吸引力。
says Kudla 是引述句，交代评价来源。

sentences.sentence: 'A little bit strong.
sentences.translation: “味道有点浓。
difficult_points: 这是引语中的省略句，完整意思可理解为 They are a little bit strong。
strong 在描述食物风味时表示味道浓烈，而不是“强壮”。

sentences.sentence: And very aromatic.'
sentences.translation: 而且非常芳香。”
difficult_points: And 连接前一句引语中的另一个省略评价，承接 They are 的省略主谓。
aromatic 描述食物香气浓郁，与前面的 strong 一起评价风味。

sentences.sentence: The team in China re-domesticated several strains of wild tomatoes with desirable traits lost in domesticated tomatoes.
sentences.translation: 中国团队重新驯化了几个带有理想性状的野生番茄品系，而这些性状已在驯化番茄中丢失。

structure.text: re-domesticated several strains of wild tomatoes
with desirable traits lost in domesticated tomatoes

difficult_points: with desirable traits 修饰 several strains of wild tomatoes，说明这些野生品系具有理想性状。
lost in domesticated tomatoes 是过去分词短语，修饰 traits。
re-domesticated 表示“重新驯化”，与文章主题呼应。

sentences.sentence: In this way they managed to create a strain resistant to a common disease called bacterial spot race, which can devastate yields.
sentences.translation: 通过这种方式，他们成功培育出一个能够抵抗一种名为细菌性斑点病的常见病害的品系，而这种病害会严重破坏产量。

structure.text: managed to create a strain resistant to a common disease called bacterial spot race
resistant to a common disease called bacterial spot race

difficult_points: In this way 指代前一句的重新驯化野生番茄品系的方法。
resistant to ... 是形容词短语，后置修饰 a strain。
called bacterial spot race 是过去分词短语，说明 disease 的名称。
which 引导非限制性定语从句，补充说明该病害会破坏产量。

sentences.sentence: They also created another strain that is more salt tolerant – and has higher levels of vitamin C.
sentences.translation: 他们还培育出另一个品系，它更耐盐，而且维生素C含量更高。

structure.text: and has higher levels of vitamin C

difficult_points: that 引导定语从句，修饰 another strain。
is more salt tolerant 和 has higher levels of vitamin C 是并列谓语，共用关系代词 that 的先行词。
破折号前后形成补充说明，强调该品系还有更高维生素C含量。

sentences.sentence: Meanwhile, Joyce Van Eck at the Boyce Thompson Institute in New York state decided to use the same approach to domesticate the groundcherry or goldenberry (Physalis pruinosa) for the first time.
sentences.translation: 与此同时，纽约州Boyce Thompson研究所的Joyce Van Eck决定用同样的方法首次驯化地樱桃或金莓（Physalis pruinosa）。

structure.text: Joyce Van Eck at the Boyce Thompson Institute in New York state
decided to use the same approach
to domesticate the groundcherry or goldenberry (Physalis pruinosa) for the first time

difficult_points: Meanwhile 表示同时期的另一项研究，承接前文两个番茄团队。
decided to use ... to domesticate ... 中第一个不定式作 decided 的宾语，第二个说明使用方法的目的。
the groundcherry or goldenberry 是同一植物的两个名称，括号中给出学名。
for the first time 强调这种植物此前未被驯化。

sentences.sentence: This fruit looks similar to the closely related Cape gooseberry (Physalis peruviana).
sentences.translation: 这种水果看起来与亲缘关系很近的秘鲁酸浆（Physalis peruviana）相似。

structure.text: looks similar to the closely related Cape gooseberry (Physalis peruviana)

difficult_points: This fruit 指代上一句的 groundcherry or goldenberry。
looks similar to 是系动词加形容词短语，表示外观相似。
closely related 修饰 Cape gooseberry，说明两者亲缘接近。

sentences.sentence: Groundcherries are already sold to a limited extent in the US but they are hard to produce because the plant has a sprawling growth habit and the small fruits fall off the branches when ripe.
sentences.translation: 地樱桃已经在美国有少量销售，但它们很难生产，因为这种植物有蔓生的生长习性，而且小果实成熟时会从枝条上脱落。

structure.text: are already sold to a limited extent in the US
but they are hard to produce
because the plant has a sprawling growth habit and the small fruits fall off the branches when ripe

difficult_points: are already sold 是被动语态，说明地樱桃已被销售。
but 连接转折，指出虽已销售但生产困难。
because 从句中 has 和 fall off 是并列谓语，给出两个生产困难的原因。
when ripe 是省略结构，完整可理解为 when they are ripe。

sentences.sentence: Van Eck's team has edited the plants to increase fruit size, make their growth more compact and to stop fruits dropping.
sentences.translation: Van Eck的团队已经编辑了这些植物，以增大果实、让植株生长更紧凑，并阻止果实掉落。

structure.text: to increase fruit size, make their growth more compact and to stop fruits dropping

difficult_points: has edited 使用现在完成时，强调编辑工作已经完成并产生当前结果。
to increase ..., make ... and to stop ... 是并列目的结构，说明编辑植物的三个目标。
stop fruits dropping 中 fruits dropping 是动名词复合结构，作 stop 的宾语。
their 指代 the plants，说明植物生长形态变得更紧凑。

sentences.sentence: 'There's potential for this to be a commercial crop,' says Van Eck.
sentences.translation: Van Eck说：“这有潜力成为一种商业作物。”

structure.text: for this to be a commercial crop,'

difficult_points: There's potential for ... to ... 表示“……有潜力做某事”。
this 指代经过编辑改良后的地樱桃。
commercial crop 指可商业化种植和销售的作物，而不只是实验植物。

sentences.sentence: But she adds that taking the work further would be expensive because of the need to pay for a licence for the CRISPR technology and get regulatory approval.
sentences.translation: 但她补充说，进一步推进这项工作会很昂贵，因为需要为CRISPR技术支付许可费用，并获得监管批准。

structure.text: that taking the work further would be expensive
because of the need to pay for a licence for the CRISPR technology and get regulatory approval
to pay for a licence for the CRISPR technology and get regulatory approval

difficult_points: that 引导宾语从句，说明 Van Eck 补充的内容。
taking the work further 是动名词短语，在从句中作主语。
would be expensive 表示进一步工作将会成本很高。
because of 后接名词短语 the need，后面的不定式解释 need 的具体内容。

sentences.sentence: This approach could boost the use of many obscure plants, says Jonathan Jones of the Sainsbury Lab in the UK.
sentences.translation: 英国Sainsbury实验室的Jonathan Jones说，这种方法可能会促进许多不知名植物的利用。

structure.text: could boost the use of many obscure plants
says Jonathan Jones of the Sainsbury Lab in the UK

difficult_points: This approach 指代利用 CRISPR 快速驯化植物的方法。
could boost 表示可能促进，不是确定已经发生。
says Jonathan Jones ... 是插入式报道来源，主干仍是 This approach could boost ...。

sentences.sentence: But it will be hard for new foods to grow so popular with farmers and consumers that they become new staple crops, he thinks.
sentences.translation: 但他认为，要让新食物在农民和消费者中变得如此受欢迎，以至于成为新的主粮作物，将会很难。

structure.text: for new foods to grow so popular with farmers and consumers that they become new staple crops
so popular with farmers and consumers that they become new staple crops

difficult_points: it 是形式主语，真正的主语是 for new foods to grow ... 这个不定式复合结构。
so ... that ... 是结果结构，表示受欢迎到足以成为新主粮作物。
they 指代 new foods。
he thinks 放在句末，说明这是 Jonathan Jones 的判断。

sentences.sentence: The three teams already have their eye on other plants that could be 'catapulted into the mainstream', including foxtail, oat-grass and cowpea.
sentences.translation: 这三个团队已经盯上了其他可能被“迅速推向主流”的植物，包括狗尾草、燕麦草和豇豆。

structure.text: already have their eye on other plants
that could be 'catapulted into the mainstream'

difficult_points: have their eye on 表示“关注、盯上”，不是字面上的眼睛在某物上。
that 引导定语从句，修饰 other plants。
could be 'catapulted into the mainstream' 是被动语态，形象表达这些植物可能迅速进入主流。
including 后列举 other plants 的具体例子。

sentences.sentence: By choosing wild plants that are drought or heat tolerant, says Gao, we could create crops that will thrive even as the planet warms.
sentences.translation: 高说，通过选择耐旱或耐热的野生植物，我们可以培育出即使在地球变暖时也能茁壮生长的作物。

structure.text: By choosing wild plants that are drought or heat tolerant
that are drought or heat tolerant
could create crops that will thrive even as the planet warms
that will thrive even as the planet warms

difficult_points: By choosing ... 是方式状语，说明创造耐候作物的方法。
第一个 that 引导定语从句，修饰 wild plants；第二个 that 修饰 crops。
says Gao 插入句中，交代观点来源，不影响主句 we could create crops。
even as the planet warms 表示即便地球变暖这一背景持续发生。

sentences.sentence: But Kudla didn't want to reveal which species were in his team's sights, because CRISPR has made the process so easy.
sentences.translation: 但Kudla不愿透露他的团队瞄准了哪些物种，因为CRISPR已经让这个过程变得非常容易。

structure.text: which species were in his team's sights
because CRISPR has made the process so easy

difficult_points: which species were in his team's sights 是宾语从句，作 reveal 的宾语。
be in one's sights 是比喻表达，表示成为目标或关注对象。
because 引导原因状语从句，解释不愿透露的原因。
has made the process so easy 是现在完成时，强调 CRISPR 已经改变了驯化过程的难度。

expressions.meaning_zh: 在他的团队目标范围内
让这个过程变得很容易

sentences.sentence: 'Anyone with the right skills could go to their lab and do this.'
sentences.translation: “任何具备相应技能的人都可以去自己的实验室做这件事。”

structure.text: could go to their lab and do this
go to their lab and do this

difficult_points: Anyone with the right skills 表示条件很宽泛，只要有相关技能的人都可能做到。
could 表示能力或可能性，呼应前一句 CRISPR 让流程变容易。
go to their lab and do this 是并列动词结构，this 指代利用 CRISPR 进行快速驯化。
